concepts.mindmap.p: This mind-map presents an structural view of the essential technical, operational, and technological aspects of wind energy, serving as a complete reference for planning, training, and decision‑making.

dfigb2b.p1: This program computes all the magnetic and electric variable on the Asynchronous Induction Machine used of the Doubly Fed Induction Generator family of Wind Turbine, and also the demand placed on the Back-to->Back converter. Ideal for post-morten analysis of these converters.
dfigb2b.p2: This video contains a brief explanation.

guided.experiments1.p: In this Tutorial there are a list of experiments that illustrate key concepts, and comparisons between types of wind turbines families.

nrel.access.h3: Acces to the National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL) documentation
nrel.access.p1: NREL published in March 2008 this reference paper: "Advanced Control Design for Wind Turbines Part I: Control Design, Implementation, and Initial Test - Part I: Control Design, Implementation, and Initial Tests".
nrel.access.p2: It contains the description of algorithms required for designing Drive Trains that extract the maximum power from the existing wind.
opcurve.button: ▶ Execute 'Operational Curve of Variable Speed Wind Turbines'
opcurve.h3: Operational Curve of Variable Speed Wind Turbines
opcurve.p1: To get the maxmimum power from the existing wind speed, a technique known as Maximum Power Point Tracking algorithm (or MPPT) Blades must rotated with a velocity proportional to the Wind Speed.
opcurve.p2: This applications permits the evaluation of algorithms that must be implemented to achieve it.
opcurve.p3: It is an engineering tool designed to analyze and define the operating conditions of a wind turbine DriveTrain based on blade type, blade length, and its coupling with the electrical generator, including synchronous speed and the selected gearbox ratio.
scada.guide.title: Guided exploration of the Wind Farm Control Center/SCADA
scada.guide.step1.h3: 1 - Basic characteristics of the Electric Grid
scada.guide.step2.h3: 2 - Structure and Functionalities of a Wind Farm
scada.guide.step3.p1: Each Wind Turbine operates as an Autonomous Independent Production Unit and is capable of generating electrical power without relying on any external sensing devices, provided that the required operating conditions are met.
scada.guide.step3.p2: They admit remote command for starting production (cut in), stopping production (cut out), and (modern ones) for fixing the maximum production.
scada.guide.step4.h3: 4 - Connection of the Wind Farm with the Grid
scada.guide.step4.p1: The Wind Turbines are connected with the Substation through Medium Voltage power cables.
scada.guide.step4.p2: In on-shore Wind Farms the Substation is connected to the Grid through a High Voltage AC line (> 100 KV)
scada.guide.step4.p3: In off-shore Wind Farms this connection may be with very high voltage DC cables.
scada.guide.step5.h3: 5 - Grid Codes of the Wind Turbines
scada.guide.step5.p1: Each national Transmission System Operator (TSO) defines a mandatory Grid Code specifying the technical requirements that wind turbines must comply with when connected to the electrical network. These requirements are established to ensure system stability and continuity of supply (i.e., preventing unintended outages). The applicable Grid Code varies from one country to another.
simulator.dfigwt.p: Real-Time Simulator for the family of DFIG WTs including the simulation of the Power Electronics Back-to-Back converter.
simulator.dfigwt.title: For the Doubly-Fed Induction Generator (DFIG) family of WTs
simulator.dsaswt.p: Real-Time Simulator for the family of WTs with variable Pitch blades, and control using Active-Stall principle, and two operative Hub rotational speeds.
simulator.dsaswt.title: For the Squirrel Cage generator, Active Stall Controlled, Two-Speed family of WTs
simulator.rrcwt.p: Real-Time Simulator for the family of WTs that implements control through two mechanism: the Blades Pitch and the Winding Rotor Resistance of the asynchronous induction generator.
simulator.rrcwt.title: For the Rotor Resistance Controlled family of WTs
simulator.wfscada.button: ▶ Run the "Wind Farm Control Center/SCADA" Simulator
simulator.wfscada.p: This simulator, together with its Tutorial, enables users to understand the Wind Farm components, their operational roles, and the way they interact with one another and with the Electric Grid.

turbinefamilies.title: Families of Wind Turbines in Wind Farms

tutorial.wfsscada.button: 📖 Open the 'Wind Farm Control Center/SCADA' Simulator's Tutorial
tutorials.dfigwt.button: 📖 Open the 'Real-Time Doubly-Fed Induction Generator WT Simulator' Tutorial
tutorials.dfigwt.h3: Tutorial of the 'Real-Time Doubly-Fed Induction Generator WT Simulator'
tutorials.dfigwt.p1: This WT family constitutes the vast majority of the WTs installed on Wind Farms, world-wide.
tutorials.dfigwt.p2: It is a complex production plant that exploit the capabilities of modern Power Electronics.
tutorials.directaccess.p: The Tutorials are a key component of the product line of ACM SL. They normally are associated to the Real-Time Simulators and Applications, and can be access from whitin them: here you can access them directly.
tutorials.directaccess.title: Direct access to the Tutorials and Practical Classes
tutorials.dsaswt.button: 📖 Open the 'Real-Time Two-Speed Active Stall WT Simulator' Tutorial
tutorials.dsaswt.h3: Tutorial of the 'Real-Time Two-Speed Active Stall WT Simulator'
tutorials.dsaswt.p1: In this tutorial you will find the description of:
tutorials.dsaswt.p2: * the most common subsystems in a modern WT
tutorials.dsaswt.p3: * the description of its working conditions
tutorials.dsaswt.p4: * the basis of the asynchronous induction generator
tutorials.dsasex.button: 📖 Open the Practical Classes of the 'Real-Time Two-Speed Active Stall WT Simulator'
tutorials.dsasex.h3: Practical Classes with the 'Real-Time Two-Speed Active Stall WT Simulator'
tutorials.dsasex.p1: These practical classes provide guided exercises with the simulator, analysing the wind turbine behaviour in its main operating conditions:
tutorials.dsasex.p2: * Cut-in with low wind, generator speed changes (small/large generator), wind direction effects, controller state machine monitoring, hydraulic brake circuit monitoring, grid fault scenarios and strong wind behaviour.
tutorials.ldpframework.p1: All the Real-Time simulators of ACMSL are based on the framework LdP, developed by ACM SL, that poses unique functionalities for the development of Real-Time Simulators, that, in turn, are based on Digital Twins
tutorials.ldpframework.p2: In this tutorial you have a description of its characteristics.
tutorials.rrcwt.button: 📖 Open the 'Real-Time Rotor Resistance Controlled WT Simulator' Tutorial
tutorials.rrcwt.h3: Tutorial of the 'Real-Time Rotor Resistance Controlled WT Simulator'
tutorials.rrcwt.p1: This WT family constitutes an important step in the development of modern WTs, like DFIGs.
tutorials.rrcwt.p2: It uses a common characteristic of the Asynchronous Induction Generators to solve the problem of using the Drive Train as an energy storage device to cope with the problems associated with wind gusts.
tutorials.windfarm_scada.button: 📖 Open the 'Wind Farm Control Center/SCADA' Tutorial
tutorials.windfarm_scada.h3: Access to the 'Wind Farm Control Center/SCADA' Simulator' Tutorial
tutorials.windfarm_scada.p1: While the Wind Farm Control Center/SCADA Simulator includes built‑in access to its tutorial, this option lets users open the tutorial separately, without launching the simulator itself.
tutorials.windfarm_scada.p2: And about the main characteristics of the Electic Grid affecting the operation of the Wind Farm.

windfarms.p: Wind farms serve as the fundamental production units, supplying their generated power directly to the Transmission Tetwork of the Electric Grid.
